title: LIVE Q1 Results Today 24th July: Shriram Finance, TCP, BoB, Hindustan Zinc, ACC, Dalmia, Jindal Steel, Lodha, SBI Life, SBI Cards, CG Power, NTPC, SAIL, KFin Tech, Sapphire Foods, Welspun Corp Q1 resu
text:
Businessman and customer home buyer had achieve target means together and signed in the contract of sale of real estate | Photo Credit: Jakraphong Pongpotganatam

Q1 Results Today, July 24, 2026, Live Updates: Get real-time Q1 FY27 earnings updates, profit growth, revenue numbers and management outlook of NTPC, Shriram Finance, Hindustan Zinc, SBI Life Insurance Company, CG Power and Industrial Solutions, Bank of Baroda, Lodha Developers, Tata Consumer Products, Jindal Steel & more.

GAINERS:Cipla, Sona BLW, ABDL, MphasiS, IEX

LOSERS: Infosys, IndiGo, Meesho, Chennai Petro, Motilal OFS, Fractal

Scroll down for more updates from businessline

Motilal Oswal Financial Services fell 8% to Rs 862.10 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 839.90 from the previous close of Rs 940.25.

It reported standalone PAT at Rs 664.97 crore in Q1FY27 as against Rs 532.14 crore in Q1FY26.

Fractal Analytics stock traded 6% lower at Rs 811.20 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 795 from the previous close of Rs 861.45.

It posted a 97.8 per cent jump in its consolidated profit to Rs 74.2 crore in the first quarter.

Company received communication from Senseforth AI Research Private Limited, informing that Fractal Frontiers Inc. has been dissolved with effect from July 21, 2026.

Sona BLW Precision Forging stock scaled to a 52-week high of Rs 741.65 on the NSE after hitting a low of Rs 705 from the previous close of Rs 719.75.

It posted a standalone PAT of Rs 220.1 crore in Q1FY27 as against Rs 120 cr in Q1FY26.

Meesho shares dip 6% despite narrowed Q1 loss and optimistic long-term growth guidance of 25% CAGR over five years.

Here’s what brokerages say | ​READ MORE​

At 12 noon, Sensex traded 621.82 pts or 0.81% lower at 75,769.57; and Nifty 50 declined 175.85 pts or 0.74% to 23,693.75.

All sectoral indices except media and FMCG in red. Auto stocks lead decliners.

ABDL shares were up 1% to Rs 608.45 on the NSE at 11.41 am, hitting a high of Rs 618.80 from the previous close of Rs 602.55 . During the session, the stock touched a low of Rs 580.

Its standalone PAT was up to Rs 68.19 cr in Q1FY27 as against Rs 60.91 cr in Q1 FY26.

Earnings today: ACC, Alldigi Tech, Apar Industries, Arihant Capital Markets, Automotive Stampings and Assemblies, Associated Alcohols & Breweries, Atul, Bank of Baroda, CG Power and Industrial Solutions, CONCOR, CreditAccess Grameen, Dalmia Bharat, DCB Bank, Dolphin Offshore Enterprises (India), Ganesh Housing, Greenply Industries, Grindwell Norton, Jindal Steel, KFin Tech, Kross, Dr. Lal PathLabs, Laurus Labs, LMW, Moschip Tech, The New India Assurance Co, NTPC, Rajratan Global Wire, Ramkrishna Forgings, Sapphire Foods, SBI Cards and Payment Services, SBI Life Insurance Co, Shakti Pumps, Share India Securities, Shree Digvijay Cement, Shriram Finance, Sterlite Tech, TANFAC Industries, Tata Consumer Products, V-Mart Retail, Welspun Corp, Wendt (India), ZF Commercial Vehicle Control Systems India

InterGlobe Aviation traded 2% lower at Rs 4,905 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 4,886 from the previous close of Rs 5,023.50. The stock declined after the company reported a consolidated net loss of ₹238 crore in Q1FY27.

Cipla shares rise 3.7% despite a significant Q1 profit decline, but brokerages express caution regarding future performance.

Benchmarks down over 1% . At 10.24 am, Sensex traded 867.06 pts or 1.14% lower at 75,524.33 (hitting a low of 75,474.43); Nifty 50 fell 248.45 pts or 1.04% to 23,621.15.

MphasiS stock traded flat at Rs 2,230 on the NSE at 10.20 am after hitting a high of Rs 2340 from the previous close of Rs 2,232.40.

It reported standalone PAT of Rs 459.2 crore in Q1YF27 as against Rs 298.2 crore in Q1FY26.

Infosys shares drop over 2% post-Q1 results as brokerages cut target prices amid lowered growth outlook.

Sensex traded 659.53 pts or 0.86% lower at 75,731.86 after opening at 75,708.19 from the previous close of 76,391.39; and Nifty 50 fell 184.95 pts or 0.77% to 23,684.65.

THYROCARE TECH: Q1 CONS NET PROFIT 522M RUPEES VS 389M (YOY)

THYROCARE TECH: Q1 REVENUE 2.4B RUPEES VS 1.93B (YOY)

THYROCARE TECH: Q1 EBITDA 772M RUPEES VS 577.7M (YOY) || Q1 EBITDA MARGIN 32.18% VS 29.93% (YOY)

EBITDA Margin 12.71% vs 13.48% YoY & 11.61% QoQ

INDIAN ENERGY EXCHANGE LIMITED (IEX) Q1FY27 :-

EBITDA Margin 82.93% vs 81.35% YoY & 85.69% QoQ

* Net Loss: ₹132.84 Cr vs Loss of ₹289.36 Cr YoY vs Loss of ₹166.35 Cr QoQ | Loss Narrows YoY & QoQ

* Revenue: ₹3,712.81 Cr | Up 48% YoY | Up 5% QoQ

* EBITDA Loss: ₹111.05 Cr vs Loss of ₹138.26 Cr YoY vs Loss of ₹139.07 Cr QoQ | Loss Narrows YoY & QoQ

* EBITDA Margin: -2.99% vs -5.52% YoY vs -3.94% QoQ | Improved 253 bps YoY | Improved 95 bps QoQ

Allied Blenders and Distillers Ltd Q1FY27 Results:-

Revenue (Net of Excise duty) 978.94 Cr vs 922.86 Cr

EBITDA Margin 11.80% vs 12.09% YoY & 16.80% QoQ

PBT Ex-Exceptional Items 67.84 Cr vs 75.64 Cr

PAT After Minority interest 49.22 Cr vs 56.56 Cr

Other Income 4.64 Cr vs 7.09 Cr YoY & 12.94 Cr QoQ

In Q1 FY27, the Company reported consolidated operating revenue of Rs 912.5 Cr, a growth of 20% year on year (YoY). Revenue growth was led by the company’s Healthcare and Life Sciences (HLS) industry, which clocked 69% growth YoY. Strong sustained growth in HLS over the last several quarters has resulted in it becoming the second largest industry in the portfolio. Banking, Financial Services and Insurance (BFSI) also performed very well, growing 36% YoY in Q1. Fractal’s largest industry, Consumer Packaged Goods and Retail (CPGR), continued to gather momentum, growing 19% YoY.

Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd. (MOFSL) reported its highest-ever quarterly Total PAT of ₹1,513 Cr including Other Comprehensive Income (OCI) in Q1FY27. This is led by strong growth in Asset Management business.

Asset Management (AMC & MO Alternates) PAT grew by 73% YoY to ₹ 245Cr in Q1 FY27 and is now the largest contributor to PAT at 40%. Total Asset Under Management (AUM) grew by 31% on YoY basis at ₹2.12 lakh Cr
